2605.23238.title: GENSTRAT: Toward a Science of Strategic Reasoning in Large Language Models
2605.23238.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as economic agents in marketplaces, auctions, and bidding settings. Anticipating their behavior in any specific deployment is hard. Existing strategic-reasoning benchmarks evaluate models on fixed canonical games. These benchmarks may saturate as the frontier improves, and they do not allow evaluators to generalize with confidence from benchmark performance to the varied and messy strategic environments that actual deployments involve. We introduce GENSTRAT, which uses procedurally generated strategic environments to address these challenges. Concretely, we generate a distribution of two-player zero-sum imperfect-information card games. The generator can draw fresh games on demand, allowing for evergreen evaluation and resistance to contamination. We pair the game distribution with a capability-profile methodology that decomposes model competence across six axes (state space, temporal depth, information sensitivity, opponent modeling, risk, and brittleness). We also introduce a jaggedness measure of within-distribution smoothness that detects when a model's advantage jumps unpredictably between strategically similar games. We sample 50 benchmark games from a 2,000-game generated pool and evaluate nine frontier and open-weight LLMs in a head-to-head tournament with over 36,000 matches. Newer frontier-tier models score higher on average. Beyond that average, models with near-identical overall strength show qualitatively different capability profiles, and two of the top three leaderboard models (gpt-5 and claude) are noticeably more locally volatile than the third (gemini-3.1-pro), despite being close in overall strength. Together, the capability profile and the jaggedness measure give a deployment-relevant diagnostic that the overall ranking alone cannot provide.
2605.23238.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作市場、拍賣和競標環境中的經濟代理人。預測它們在任何特定部署中的行為是困難的。現有的戰略推理基準在固定的典範遊戲上評估模型。隨著前沿的改善，這些基準可能會飽和，並且它們不允許評估者自信地從基準性能推廣到實際部署所涉及的多樣且複雜的戰略環境。我們引入了GENSTRAT，它使用程序生成的戰略環境來解決這些挑戰。具體而言，我們生成一個兩人零和不完全信息卡牌遊戲的分佈。生成器可以按需生成新的遊戲，允許持久的評估和抵抗污染。我們將遊戲分佈與能力輪廓方法相結合，該方法在六個軸（狀態空間、時間深度、信息敏感性、對手建模、風險和脆弱性）上分解模型的能力。我們還引入了一種鋸齒度度量，來測量分佈內的平滑度，檢測模型的優勢在戰略上相似的遊戲之間不穩定地跳躍。我們從2,000個生成的遊戲池中抽取50個基準遊戲，並在一場超過36,000場比賽的對抗賽中評估九個前沿和開放權重的LLMs。較新的前沿級模型平均得分更高。超出該平均值的模型在整體強度上幾乎相同，但顯示出質量上不同的能力輪廓，三個排行榜前列模型中的兩個（gpt-5和claude）在局部波動性上明顯高於第三個（gemini-3.1-pro），儘管整體強度接近。總體而言，能力輪廓和鋸齒度度量提供了一個與部署相關的診斷，這是僅靠整體排名無法提供的。
2605.23238.author: Vartan Shadarevian et.al.
2605.23238.authors: Vartan Shadarevian, Kia Ghods, Alex Kenich, Anany Kotawala
